directory_structure.description: Skills are organized by functional domain (SDD workflow stages) rather than by technology

files: engram-convention.md - Naming and recovery for Engram persistence
openspec-convention.md - File structure for OpenSpec persistence

sdd-*/ (8 skills).structure: Each skill has only SKILL.md (no code, only documentation)

skills_catalog.name: sdd-init
skills_catalog.purpose: Initialize SDD context in any project - detects stack, conventions, bootstraps persistence backend
skills_catalog.trigger: When user wants to initialize SDD in a project (sdd init, iniciar sdd, openspec init)

outputs.openspec_mode: Creates openspec/ directory structure with config.yaml

key_responsibilities: Detect project tech stack (package.json, go.mod, pyproject.toml, etc)
Detect testing framework and CI conventions
Create openspec/config.yaml with detected context (if openspec mode)
Persist project context for dependency by other SDD skills

dependencies.incoming: None - this is the entry point
dependencies.outgoing: All other SDD skills depend on sdd-init for project context

skills_catalog.config_integration: Reads project's existing linters, formatters, CI to auto-populate rules

skills_catalog.name: sdd-explore
skills_catalog.purpose: Explore and investigate ideas before committing to a change - think through problems, compare approaches
skills_catalog.trigger: When orchestrator launches to think through feature, investigate codebase, or clarify requirements (/sdd-explore <topic>)

outputs.always: Structured analysis markdown with approaches, pros/cons, recommendation

key_responsibilities: Read and understand the request (new feature, bug fix, refactor)
Investigate codebase thoroughly - entry points, affected files, existing behavior
Analyze multiple approaches if they exist
Compare approaches with pros/cons/complexity
Make recommendation with risks
Optionally save exploration to file if change name provided

file_paths.reads: Entire codebase (searches for patterns, related functionality, tests)
file_paths.writes_none: Returns inline only if no change_name

skills_catalog.special_notes: This is a DISCOVERY phase - does NOT modify code, only analyzes. Can be standalone (/sdd-explore) or tied to a change (/sdd-new)

skills_catalog.name: sdd-propose
skills_catalog.purpose: Create a change proposal with intent, scope, and approach
skills_catalog.trigger: When orchestrator launches to create/update proposal for a change (/sdd-new <change-name>)
key_responsibilities: Read existing specs if they exist (understand current behavior being modified)
Create change directory structure (if openspec mode)
Write proposal.md with: Intent, Scope, Approach, Affected Areas, Risks, Rollback Plan, Dependencies, Success Criteria
Every proposal MUST have rollback plan
Every proposal MUST have success criteria
skills_catalog.contract_notes: Must follow persistence-contract.md - NEVER force openspec creation unless explicitly requested

skills_catalog.name: sdd-spec
skills_catalog.purpose: Write specifications with requirements and scenarios (delta specs for changes)
skills_catalog.trigger: When orchestrator launches to write/update specs for a change
key_responsibilities: Identify affected domains from proposal's 'Affected Areas'
Read existing spec.md if it exists (understand current behavior)
Write delta specs: ADDED Requirements, MODIFIED Requirements, REMOVED Requirements
If no existing spec, write FULL spec (not delta)
Every requirement MUST have at least ONE scenario
Use Given/When/Then format for scenarios
Use RFC 2119 keywords (MUST, SHALL, SHOULD, MAY) for requirement strength

file_paths.reads_engram: Retrieves proposal artifact via mem_search + mem_get_observation

skills_catalog.critical_rule: Never include implementation details - specs describe WHAT, not HOW

skills_catalog.name: sdd-design
skills_catalog.purpose: Create technical design document with architecture decisions and approach
skills_catalog.trigger: When orchestrator launches to write/update technical design for a change
key_responsibilities: Read actual codebase that will be affected - entry points, modules, patterns
Read proposal and spec (understand WHAT)
Write design.md describing HOW with: Technical Approach, Architecture Decisions, Data Flow, File Changes, Interfaces/Contracts, Testing Strategy, Migration Plan
Every decision MUST have rationale
Include concrete file paths, not abstract descriptions
Follow existing project patterns (not generic best practices)

dependencies.incoming: proposal (required), spec (optional - spec may not exist yet if running in parallel)

skills_catalog.critical_rule: MUST read actual codebase before designing - never guess

skills_catalog.name: sdd-tasks
skills_catalog.purpose: Break down a change into an implementation task checklist
skills_catalog.trigger: When orchestrator launches to create/update task breakdown for a change

outputs.openspec: File: openspec/changes/{change-name}/tasks.md (updated by sdd-apply as tasks complete)

key_responsibilities: Analyze design to identify all file changes and dependencies
Determine correct implementation order
Write tasks.md with hierarchical phases: Foundation, Core Implementation, Integration, Testing, Cleanup
Each task MUST be: Specific (what file/method), Actionable (how to do it), Verifiable (how to test), Small (one session)
Use hierarchical numbering: 1.1, 1.2, 2.1, 2.2
Reference concrete file paths
Order tasks by dependency - Phase 1 shouldn't depend on Phase 2
skills_catalog.tdd_integration: If TDD mode detected, integrate test-first tasks: RED task (write failing test) → GREEN task (make it pass) → REFACTOR task

skills_catalog.name: sdd-apply
skills_catalog.purpose: Implement tasks from the change, writing actual code following specs and design
skills_catalog.trigger: When orchestrator launches to implement one or more tasks from a change

outputs.openspec: Updates openspec/changes/{change-name}/tasks.md with [x] marks, creates actual code files

key_responsibilities: Read specs - understand WHAT the code must do
Read design - understand HOW to structure the code
Read existing code - understand current patterns
Detect implementation mode: TDD or Standard
TDD mode: RED (write failing test) → GREEN (minimal code to pass) → REFACTOR (clean up)
Standard mode: Write code directly following specs as acceptance criteria
Mark tasks complete [x] as they finish
Load and follow relevant coding skills if available (react-19, typescript, django-drf, tdd, pytest, vitest, etc)
Match project conventions and patterns

file_paths.reads_openspec: All previous artifacts, reads existing code to match patterns
file_paths.writes: Actual project code files (implementation code, test files)

tdd_workflow.detection: From openspec/config.yaml rules.apply.tdd, or project's installed skills, or existing test patterns
tdd_workflow.red_phase: Write failing test first - confirms the test is meaningful
tdd_workflow.green_phase: Write minimum code to make test pass

skills_catalog.test_runner_detection: From config.yaml rules.apply.test_command, package.json scripts.test, pyproject.toml, Makefile, etc
skills_catalog.critical_rule: ALWAYS read specs before implementing - specs are your acceptance criteria

skills_catalog.name: sdd-verify
skills_catalog.purpose: Validate that implementation matches specs, design, and tasks
skills_catalog.trigger: When orchestrator launches to verify a completed (or partially completed) change
key_responsibilities: Check completeness: Count total vs completed tasks
Check correctness: For each spec requirement/scenario, find evidence in code
Check coherence: Verify design decisions were followed
Check testing: Verify test files exist and cover scenarios
Run tests: Execute test suite (real execution, not static analysis)
Run build: Execute build/type-check commands
Run coverage: Check if configured coverage threshold is met
Spec Compliance Matrix: Cross-reference each spec scenario to test results
Generate verify-report.md with CRITICAL/WARNING/SUGGESTION issues

file_paths.reads: Source code, test files, project configs (tsconfig.json, pyproject.toml, Makefile, etc)
file_paths.executes: Tests (detected test runner), build commands, coverage analysis

verification_gates.completeness: All tasks completed? (warning if incomplete core tasks)
verification_gates.correctness: Code implements all spec requirements? (critical if missing)

skills_catalog.compliance_matrix: Each spec scenario must have: test exists + test passed = COMPLIANT
skills_catalog.critical_rule: Static analysis alone is NOT enough - ALWAYS execute tests and build

skills_catalog.name: sdd-archive
skills_catalog.purpose: Sync delta specs to main specs and archive a completed change
skills_catalog.trigger: When orchestrator launches to archive a change after implementation and verification

outputs.engram: Artifact type: archive-report, topic_key: sdd/{change-name}/archive-report (includes lineage of all observation IDs)

key_responsibilities: Read delta specs from openspec/changes/{change-name}/specs/
If main spec exists: Apply delta (ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED requirements)
If main spec doesn't exist: Copy delta as new main spec
Preserve all OTHER requirements not mentioned in delta
Move change folder to archive with date prefix (YYYY-MM-DD-{change-name})
Verify archive contains all artifacts (proposal, specs, design, tasks, verify-report)

dependencies.incoming: verify-report (required to check for CRITICAL issues before archive), all other artifacts (for audit trail)
dependencies.outgoing: None - this is the exit point of the SDD cycle

file_paths.writes: Updated main specs with merged deltas

merge_rules.matching: Match requirements by name (e.g., '### Requirement: Session Expiration')
merge_rules.preserve: Keep all OTHER requirements not in delta
merge_rules.apply: ADDED → append, MODIFIED → replace, REMOVED → delete
merge_rules.destructive_warning: If merge would remove large sections, WARN orchestrator and ask for confirmation

skills_catalog.audit_trail: Archive is permanent - never delete or modify archived changes

skills_catalog.name: go-testing
skills_catalog.purpose: Go testing patterns for Gentleman.Dots, including Bubbletea TUI testing
skills_catalog.trigger: When writing Go tests, using teatest, or adding test coverage
skills_catalog.inputs: N/A - this is a reference skill, not invoked by orchestrator
key_patterns: Table-driven tests (struct array with test cases)
Golden file testing (compare output against saved files)
skills_catalog.test_file_organization: model_test.go, update_test.go, view_test.go alongside source files, testdata/ for golden files

skills_catalog.name: skill-creator
skills_catalog.purpose: Creates new AI agent skills following the Agent Skills spec
skills_catalog.trigger: When user asks to create a new skill, add agent instructions, or document patterns for AI
skills_catalog.inputs: N/A - this is a reference skill, not invoked by orchestrator
when_to_create_skill: A pattern is used repeatedly and AI needs guidance
Project-specific conventions differ from generic best practices
Decision trees help AI choose the right approach

frontmatter_fields.name: Required - skill identifier (lowercase, hyphens)
frontmatter_fields.description: Required - What + Trigger in one block
frontmatter_fields.metadata.author: Required - gentleman-programming

content_guidelines: Keep code examples minimal and focused
Include Commands section with copy-paste commands
Do NOT use web URLs in references (local paths only)

skills_catalog.name: _shared (Infrastructure)
skills_catalog.purpose: Shared conventions and contracts across all SDD skills

components.purpose: Defines mode resolution (engram|openspec|none) and behavior per mode
Deterministic naming and recovery protocol for Engram persistence
content: Artifact type definitions (explore, proposal, spec, design, tasks, apply-progress, verify-report, archive-report)
Naming rules: title, topic_key, type, project, scope

components.purpose: File structure and conventions for OpenSpec persistence
content: Directory structure: openspec/, specs/, changes/, archive/

critical_contracts.naming_determinism: Artifacts named by: sdd/{change-name}/{artifact-type} - enables exact recovery and deduplication

dependency_graph.description: How skills depend on each other and their artifacts

stages.dependencies: requires: proposal, spec, design, tasks, implementation
requires: verify-report (to check for CRITICAL issues)
stages.final_action: Moves change folder to archive, merges delta specs into main specs, completes SDD cycle

dependency_graph.dag: proposal → (specs ⟷ design) → tasks → apply → verify → archive
dependency_graph.parallel_execution: sdd-spec and sdd-design can run in parallel (both depend on proposal only)

blocking_conditions.sdd-tasks: Requires ALL of proposal, spec, AND design
blocking_conditions.sdd-apply: Requires ALL of proposal, spec, design, AND tasks
blocking_conditions.sdd-verify: Requires implementation to be complete (sdd-apply done)
blocking_conditions.sdd-archive: BLOCKED if verify-report has CRITICAL issues

configuration_system.description: How skills read and respect project-specific configuration

schema.context: Project context string (tech stack, architecture, testing, style)

rules.proposal: List of proposal-specific rules (e.g., 'Include rollback plan for risky changes')
rules.specs: List of spec rules (e.g., 'Use Given/When/Then format')
rules.design: List of design rules (e.g., 'Include sequence diagrams')
rules.tasks: List of task rules (e.g., 'Group by phase')

apply.test_command: String - command to run tests (e.g., 'npm test', 'pytest')

verify.test_command: String - override test command for verification

rules.archive: List of archive rules (e.g., 'Warn before merging destructive deltas')

how_skills_use_config.sdd-init: Detects stack and creates rules based on conventions
how_skills_use_config.sdd-apply: Reads rules.apply.tdd and rules.apply.test_command to determine workflow

integration_points.orchestrator_control: The Orchestrator (LEAD AGENT) controls SDD workflow by launching sub-agents

sdd_commands.sdd new <change-name>: Launches sdd-propose sub-agent (orchestrator may first call sdd-explore)
sdd_commands.sdd ff <change-name>: Fast-forward: launches sdd-propose → sdd-spec → sdd-design → sdd-tasks in sequence
sdd_commands.sdd continue [change-name]: Orchestrator determines which phase to continue (e.g., sdd-apply next)
sdd_commands.sdd apply [change-name]: Launches sdd-apply sub-agent with task batch

orchestrator_responsibilities.decision_making: Orchestrator decides which skill to launch and when
orchestrator_responsibilities.state_tracking: Orchestrator tracks which artifacts exist (proposal, spec, design, tasks, apply-progress, verify-report, archive-report)
orchestrator_responsibilities.dependency_enforcement: Orchestrator ensures dependencies are met before launching skills
orchestrator_responsibilities.user_communication: Orchestrator shows user summaries between skill executions and asks for approval
orchestrator_responsibilities.error_handling: Orchestrator receives issues from skills and decides: fix in-place, re-launch skill, or escalate to user

memory_integration.engram_mode: Skills use Engram (mem_save, mem_search, mem_get_observation, mem_update) for persistence
memory_integration.openspec_mode: Skills use filesystem (Read, Write, Edit) for persistence
memory_integration.none_mode: Skills return results inline, orchestrator may save to memory if needed

current_issues.issue: No broken skills detected - all are well-documented
current_issues.evidence: Each SKILL.md is complete with Purpose, Execution Contract, What to Do steps

potential_issues.risk: sdd-spec and sdd-design can run in parallel - orchestrator must handle race conditions
potential_issues.mitigation: Orchestrator serializes or ensures both complete before sdd-tasks

potential_issues.risk: If openspec/config.yaml doesn't exist, skills may use wrong test commands
potential_issues.mitigation: sdd-init creates config.yaml; skills have fallback detection (package.json, Makefile, etc)

potential_issues.risk: If mode resolution fails (no Engram, no openspec/), skills fall back to 'none'
potential_issues.mitigation: Skills explicitly document fallback behavior; recommend user enable Engram or openspec

potential_issues.risk: sdd-verify executes actual tests - if project has slow or flaky tests, verify phase will be slow
potential_issues.mitigation: Skills recommend using -short flag or test sharding

error_states_and_issues.blocked_skills: None - all skills are ready for use

testing_strategy.unit_testing: N/A - these are documentation skills, not code

integration_testing.test_full_sdd_cycle: Initialize project → Explore → Propose → Spec/Design → Tasks → Apply → Verify → Archive
integration_testing.test_persistence_modes: Test each skill with engram, openspec, and none modes
integration_testing.test_dependency_chain: Verify artifacts from one skill feed correctly to next

end_to_end_testing.real_project: Run SDD on a sample project (Go, Python, TypeScript) from start to finish
end_to_end_testing.verify_outputs: Check that each artifact has correct format and content
end_to_end_testing.verify_state: Check that task checklist is accurate, implementation is complete, tests pass, specs are synced

regression_testing.test_incomplete_flows: What if sdd-apply is interrupted mid-way? (answer: orchestrator can call sdd-apply again)
